Ebon War Spear  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: RANGE PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 31
DMG: 8 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +4 WIS: +3
SV MAGIC: +5
WT: 7.0 Size: LARGE
Class: SHM
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Ocean of Tears
NPC Name

Ocean of Tears

Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
Pricey
QuoteReply
# Feb 24 2002 at 1:08 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758588779
69 posts
I spent quite a bit of cash on my shaman and never even considered a weapon for him(silly me)!

After a while of trying to haggle for a slime coated spear(i was near broke at the time) i found it hopeless and bought a crystal grinder(9 27 2 ac) for 100pp.

IMHO, I say this thing is worth not much more than 200pp(could get a better range item for less) and is not really great as a primary weapon. Mob it drops off of is not hard at all either.
▲ Page top
I love this weapon
QuoteReply
# Sep 21 2001 at 5:21 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251246757
Scholar
51 posts
When I first got this weapon I had been trying to come across one for a while and had been doing anything in my power (for a lvl 8 at the time) to get this thing. When I finally got it I was almost disapointed by the hype I'd heard when it was less powerfull than the 1hblunt I had been using at the time. However, I was fairly new to the game and didn't yet appriciate the greatness of having a +3 wis range item. Now adays my barbarian has 150wis partly due to this little beauty, and though I use a jade prod now, I've still got my war spear in the range slot and its doing it job nicely. If you're questioning if its a great weapon? In my oppinion, not really... but is it a great range slot item? I've got two words... get one!
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Sep 20 2001 at 11:17 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Edited, Fri Jan 25 09:09:15 2002
RE: good item
QuoteReply
# Aug 29 2001 at 11:22 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760931097
109 posts
Any shaman who is still using an enamelled black mace should trade up, if you can, to the knobbed war club. Same damage, less delay, and a nice wis bonus. Compared to the ebon war spear, the 7 lower delay starts to become really significant. I stopped using my EBM when I picked up the KWC, and I've never looked back.
▲ Page top
still good
QuoteReply
# May 16 2001 at 5:46 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245753599
52 posts
at lvl 45 I still trade out my Slime Coated Harpoon for this in my range slot because of the Strength and Sv Magic.
--if you never had a Slime Coated Harpoon in your primary slot I highly suggest it. Nothing like high dmg and low delay with quickness to mow down mobs. Definitly a difference over the high delay maces we use alot.
▲ Page top
good item
QuoteReply
# May 15 2001 at 9:38 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I use this item as weapon although people tell me to buy enamled black mace because of the 3 less dly and just use spear in throwing slot. I really dont care about 3 dly so i dont see wastin the pp. I recomend this to anyone good weapon and the +4str and +3wis really help out!
▲ Page top
RE: good item
QuoteReply
# May 26 2001 at 4:26 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
3 delay is huge! .5 damage per second (approx between this weapon and an EBM) may not sound like much, but believe me, it's a big difference.
▲ Page top
RE: good item
QuoteReply
# Nov 08 2001 at 8:55 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251246757
Scholar
51 posts
Three delay may make a difference but for a shaman, nothin sais lovin like a spear in the mobs tummy. I would stick with the spear over the mace for one main reason; Epic. Our Shaman epic prize is a piercing weapon, its never too early to get started learning the skill but the longer you wait the harder it is to raise it.

Example of what I mean... I am currently a lvl 35 (sorry, 34 still getting that lvl back after a run in with a necro camp in WK but thats another story) On a normal lvling day I do alot of grouping in the Overthere with whoever wants my buffs and my puppy to help them out. I don't melee much at all in groups anymore and from what I hear, we shaman do it less and less later as our buffs get better and better, the time we spend medding in a battle gets longer and longer. So, the only time I really have to pratice piercing is when I solo. I don't know about any of you but I don't want to have to rely on getting my skill up from the start when I'm lvl 35 or 40. Someday I'll have that Spear of Fate and I'll be greatfull I spent so much time getting my skill to master on piercing when I was a young shaman.

Bottom line (litterally) I'd stick with the pircing over the blunt.
▲ Page top
Not that tough
QuoteReply
# Feb 16 2001 at 5:27 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Not 1700 hp- 2 nukes and he was running- I nuke for 508- pet finished him. Hits in the 70s.

# Jan 01 2001 at 11:42 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Soarin Brightfeather on Aviak island in OOT. Supposodly a hard 35. Not sure if he is a caster or not. Also heard one would have to kill many Aviaks for him to spawn.
▲ Page top

# Jan 06 2001 at 5:03 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
He is a lvl 35 tank with roughly 1700 hp. He is easier than Kaladim residents or a specter.
